title: Upon Carthy's Threatening To Translate Pindar (Epigram Against Carthy)
content:
You have undone Horace, - what should hinder
    Thy Muse from falling upon Pindar?
    But ere you mount his fiery steed,
    Beware, O Bard, how you proceed: - 
    For should you give him once the reins,
    High up in air he'll turn your brains;
    And if you should his fury check,
    'Tis ten to one he breaks your neck.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Jonathan Swift delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Carthy's Threatening To Translate Pindar (Epigram Against Carthy)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
